text:
(1) Developing a braking force that is not less than 43.5 percent of its gross weight.

(2) Decelerating to a stop from not more than 20 miles per hour at not less than 17 feet per second.

(3) Stopping from a speed of 20 miles per hour in not more than 25 feet, such distance to be measured from the point at which movement of the service brake pedal or control begins.
markdown:
### Chapter 316. State Unif. Traffic Control
##### § 316.267 — Brakes on electric-powered vehicles

(1) Developing a braking force that is not less than 43.5 percent of its gross weight.

(2) Decelerating to a stop from not more than 20 miles per hour at not less than 17 feet per second.

(3) Stopping from a speed of 20 miles per hour in not more than 25 feet, such distance to be measured from the point at which movement of the service brake pedal or control begins.

subsections.text: Developing a braking force that is not less than 43.5 percent of its gross weight.
Decelerating to a stop from not more than 20 miles per hour at not less than 17 feet per second.
Stopping from a speed of 20 miles per hour in not more than 25 feet, such distance to be measured from the point at which movement of the service brake pedal or control begins.
